summary: React plugin (@osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget) enabling bi-directional communication between custom iframed React applications and Palantir Workshop via window.postMessage. Provides a useWorkshopContext hook that accepts a declarative config definition describing typed fields (inputOutput variables, events, and listOf groups), negotiates a handshake with the Workshop parent frame, and returns a strongly-typed context object with async-value-wrapped field values plus setter methods to push state changes back to Workshop. Supports all Workshop variable types including ObjectSet (via primaryKeys or temporaryObjectSetRid for OSDK v2+), structs (partially), dynamic iframe height control, and nested listOf configurations.

ontologyConcepts.name: OntologyObject
ontologyConcepts.description: Minimal subset of OSDK's OntologyObject containing $rid (string) and $primaryKey (string | number). Used when setting objectSet variable values from the iframe back to Workshop - the widget extracts $rid for Workshop and $primaryKey for local context storage.

ontologyConcepts.name: ObjectSetLocators
ontologyConcepts.description: Discriminated union representing an object set as a list of primary keys, either ObjectSetLocator_WithStringPKeys (type:'string', primaryKeys:string[]) or ObjectSetLocator_WithNumberPKeys (type:'number', primaryKeys:number[]). This is the format received FROM Workshop when passing objectSet variables to the iframe. Capped at 10,000 primaryKeys.

ontologyConcepts.name: ObjectRids
ontologyConcepts.description: Container for objectRids (string[]) - the format used when sending objectSet values FROM the iframe TO Workshop. Workshop prefers object RIDs for loading objects. Capped at 10,000.

ontologyConcepts.name: objectSet variableType
ontologyConcepts.description: Variable type 'objectSet' in config definitions requires objectTypeId (string, e.g. 'rotten-tomatoes-movies') identifying the concrete Ontology ObjectType. Used with @osdk/client < 2.0. Being deprecated in favor of temporaryObjectSetRid.

ontologyConcepts.name: temporaryObjectSetRid variableType
ontologyConcepts.description: Variable type 'temporaryObjectSetRid' passes a string RID that can be hydrated via OSDK v2+'s hydrateObjectSetFromRid(). Removes the 10,000 object limit. The recommended path for ObjectSet exchange between Workshop and custom widgets.

ontologyConcepts.name: objectTypeId
ontologyConcepts.description: String identifier for a concrete Ontology ObjectType, required when using 'objectSet' variable type. Maps to a specific type in the Foundry Ontology (e.g. 'rotten-tomatoes-movies').

ontologyConcepts.name: StructValue / StructValueWithObjectRids
ontologyConcepts.description: Struct variable values containing structFields: { [fieldId: string]: value | undefined }. StructValue is for reading (uses ObjectSetLocators for object sets), StructValueWithObjectRids is for writing (uses ObjectRids). Struct support is partially implemented - not yet fully supported in Workshop.

apiSurface.name: useWorkshopContext
apiSurface.signature: useWorkshopContext<T extends IConfigDefinition>(configFields: IConfigDefinition, options?: IWorkshopContextOptions): IAsyncValue<IWorkshopContext<T>> | IAsyncValue<IWorkshopContextWithHeight<T>>
apiSurface.description: Primary entry point. Accepts a const config definition array and optional options. Sends config to Workshop parent via postMessage, listens for acceptance/rejection, and returns an async-value-wrapped context object. When enableSetAutoMaxHeight is true, returns IWorkshopContextWithHeight which includes a setAutoMaxHeight method. Outside iframe, returns default values immediately.

apiSurface.name: IConfigDefinition
apiSurface.description: Array of config field definitions. Must be declared with 'as const satisfies IConfigDefinition' for TypeScript to infer literal types for fieldId properties, enabling strongly-typed context object generation.

apiSurface.name: IConfigDefinitionField
apiSurface.description: Individual field in the config. fieldId becomes the property name in the context object. field specifies 'single' (with inputOutput or event fieldValue) or 'listOf' (recursive nested config).

apiSurface.name: IWorkshopContext<T>
apiSurface.signature: { [K in T[number] as K['fieldId']]: ValueAndSetterMethods | ExecutableEvent | IWorkshopContext[] }
apiSurface.description: Strongly-typed context object computed from config definition. Each fieldId maps to: ValueAndSetterMethods (for inputOutput with fieldValue + setLoading/setLoadedValue/setReloadingValue/setFailedWithError), ExecutableEvent (for event with executeEvent method), or IWorkshopContext[] (for listOf, array of nested contexts).

apiSurface.name: ValueAndSetterMethods<T>
apiSurface.signature: { fieldValue: IAsyncValue<VariableTypeToValueType<T> | undefined>; setLoadedValue: (value) => void; setLoading: () => void; setReloadingValue: (value) => void; setFailedWithError: (error: string) => void }
apiSurface.description: Per-field context entry for inputOutput fields. fieldValue is the current async-wrapped value from Workshop. Setter methods update both local React state and notify Workshop via postMessage.

apiSurface.name: ExecutableEvent
apiSurface.description: Per-field context entry for event fields. Calling executeEvent sends a message to Workshop to trigger the configured event. Pass MouseEvent to allow Workshop to handle modifier keys (e.g., Ctrl+click opens in new tab).

apiSurface.name: IAsyncValue<V, E>
apiSurface.description: 5-state async loading discriminated union using status field ('NOT_STARTED' | undefined | 'LOADING' | 'LOADED' | 'RELOADING' | 'FAILED'). Mirrors Workshop's internal variable loading state for 1:1 consistency. Includes type guards and visitLoadingState visitor function.

apiSurface.name: visitLoadingState
apiSurface.signature: visitLoadingState<V, R, E>(state: IAsyncValue<V, E>, visitor: IAsyncValueVisitor<V, R, E>): R
apiSurface.description: Pattern-matching visitor for IAsyncValue. Provides exhaustive handling of loading/succeeded/reloading/failed states. Primary way to render UI based on async context state.

apiSurface.name: isInsideIframe
apiSurface.description: Detects whether the app is running inside an iframe. Excludes Foundry Container Service paths (local dev). Uses window.self !== window.top check with try/catch for same-origin policy.

apiSurface.name: IWorkshopContextWithHeight<T>
apiSurface.description: Extended context type returned when enableSetAutoMaxHeight option is true. Wraps the normal context in a 'context' property and adds setAutoMaxHeight(height: number) method for dynamic iframe height control.

apiSurface.name: VariableTypeToValueType<T>
apiSurface.signature: Maps variable type discriminants to their TypeScript value types
apiSurface.description: Deep conditional type mapping: string->string, boolean->boolean, number->number, date->string('yyyy-mm-dd'), timestamp->Date, objectSet->ObjectSetLocators, temporaryObjectSetRid->string, *-list->arrays, struct->recursive struct shape. Used for reading values FROM Workshop.

apiSurface.name: VariableTypeToValueTypeToSet<T>
apiSurface.signature: Maps variable type discriminants to their setter value types
apiSurface.description: Similar to VariableTypeToValueType but for setting values. Key difference: objectSet maps to OntologyObject[] (instead of ObjectSetLocators) because setters accept OSDK objects and the plugin extracts $rid/$primaryKey internally.

architecturePatterns.description: Bi-directional communication between iframe child (custom React app) and parent (Workshop) via window.postMessage / window.addEventListener('message'). Messages are discriminated unions with type field from MESSAGE_TYPES_TO_WORKSHOP and MESSAGE_TYPES_FROM_WORKSHOP enums. Security: only processes messages where event.source === window.parent.

3-phase protocol: (1) iframe sends SENDING_CONFIG with config definition + pathname on mount, (2) Workshop responds with CONFIG_ACCEPTED (providing iframeWidgetId + initial configValues) or CONFIG_REJECTED (with rejectionReason), (3) Workshop can re-request config via REQUESTING_CONFIG when config panel opens. The iframeWidgetId scopes all subsequent messages to the specific widget instance.

Config definitions declared as const literals are recursively transformed into a strongly-typed context object via conditional mapped types. Each fieldId becomes a property key; field type determines whether it gets ValueAndSetterMethods, ExecutableEvent, or nested IWorkshopContext[]. The 'as const satisfies IConfigDefinition' pattern enables TypeScript to infer literal string types for fieldIds.

All variable values are wrapped in IAsyncValue - a 5-state discriminated union (NOT_STARTED, LOADING, LOADED, RELOADING, FAILED) that mirrors Workshop's internal variable loading states. This provides 1:1 state consistency between Workshop and iframe. Setter methods (setLoading, setLoadedValue, setReloadingValue, setFailedWithError) both update local React state and notify Workshop.

ILocator is a recursive discriminated union (single | listOf) that represents the path to a value in the nested config value map tree. Used to identify which field to update when setting values. listOf locators contain configFieldId + index + nested locator, enabling arbitrarily deep nested listOf configurations.

Two parallel type mappings exist: VariableTypeToValueType (for reading FROM Workshop, objectSet -> ObjectSetLocators with primaryKeys) and VariableTypeToValueTypeToSet (for writing TO Workshop, objectSet -> OntologyObject[] with $rid/$primaryKey). Internal transform functions convert between these representations - extracting $rid for Workshop messages and $primaryKey for local context.

Config values are stored as IConfigValueMap (fieldId -> IConfigValueType) with immutable updates. createNewConfigValueMapWithValueChange recursively traverses the tree following ILocator paths and produces new map objects via spread operators, compatible with React's state update semantics.

When not inside an iframe (detected via isInsideIframe), the hook immediately returns a loaded context populated with default values from the config definition. This enables local development without Workshop, with defaultValue on each variableType serving as dev-time mock data.

typeDefinitions.name: IVariableType_WithDefaultValue
typeDefinitions.definition: Discriminated union of all supported Workshop variable types with optional defaultValues: string, boolean, number, date (Date -> 'yyyy-mm-dd'), timestamp (Date), objectSet (objectTypeId + ObjectSetLocators), temporaryObjectSetRid (string), string-list, boolean-list, number-list, date-list, timestamp-list. Struct type is defined but commented out of the union (not yet supported).

typeDefinitions.name: IConfigValueMap
typeDefinitions.definition: { [fieldId: string]: IConfigValueType } where IConfigValueType = IConfigValueType_Single { type: 'single', value: IAsyncValue<IVariableValue | undefined> } | IConfigValueType_ListOf { type: 'listOf', listOfValues: IConfigValueMap[] }. The runtime value storage format passed between Workshop and iframe in messages.

typeDefinitions.name: ILocator
typeDefinitions.definition: ILocator_Single { type: 'single', configFieldId: string } | ILocator_ListOf { type: 'listOf', configFieldId: string, index: number, locator: ILocator }. Recursive tree path for addressing nested config values.

typeDefinitions.name: IVariableValue / IVariableToSet
typeDefinitions.definition: IVariableValue = SingleVariableValue | SingleVariableValue[] where SingleVariableValue = boolean | number | string | Date | ObjectSetLocators | StructValue. IVariableToSet replaces ObjectSetLocators with ObjectRids and StructValue with StructValueWithObjectRids for the Workshop-bound direction.

typeDefinitions.name: IStructVariableFieldTypes_WithDefaultValue
typeDefinitions.definition: { fieldId: string, fieldType: IStructVariableFieldType_WithDefaultValue }. Struct fields support all variable types except nested structs. Used in struct variable type definitions.

typeDefinitions.name: IConfigurationFieldType
typeDefinitions.definition: IConfigDefinitionFieldType_Single { type: 'single', fieldValue: IConfigDefinitionFieldType, label: string, helperText?: string } | IConfigDefinitionFieldType_ListOf { type: 'listOf', config: IConfigDefinition, label: string, helperText?: string, addButtonText?: string }

typeDefinitions.name: IConfigDefinitionFieldType
typeDefinitions.definition: IConfigDefinitionFieldType_InputOutput { type: 'inputOutput', variableType: IVariableType_WithDefaultValue } | IConfigDefinitionFieldType_Event { type: 'event' }
